On #TheProductPodcast with @villaumbrosia, she shares PM fundamentals that hold from consumer to enterprise, plus her AI strategy for regulated SMBs

suggested_reply: Fundamentals matter, but "PM fundamentals that hold from consumer to enterprise" misses the core problem: consumer PMs optimize for engagement. Enterprise PMs optimize for retention. They're solving opposite games. The crossover playbook doesn't exist.

https://t.co/6vu16MlcnL https://t.co/Dghom6PDAI
suggested_reply:
Observability platforms solving the wrong problem for most teams.

They're optimising for alert volume when the real bottleneck is: which alerts actually matter to my business outcome right now?

AI here isn't just faster alerts. It's filtering signal from noise.

their_tweet:
One large psilocybin dose beat nicotine patches by 6x odds for smoking cessation.

82 otherwise-healthy cigarette smokers, 42 received a single high-dose 30mg/70kg psilocybin session, and 40 initiated an 8- to 10-week course of nicotine patch treatment.

At 6 months; participants https://t.co/eOsIHsuwMA
suggested_reply:
Psilocybin: 1 session, 6x better outcomes.

Nicotine patches: 10 weeks of compliance theatre.

Meanwhile, most people can't stick with a £8/month vitamin D supplement for 3 weeks.

The problem isn't the molecule. It's that we've built health systems around things nobody actually uses.
